summary: Added pp:happy-args annotations (realistic --symbol/--firm/--crd/--file/--sub-product values) to all 12 hand-written novel/absorbed commands, and pp:requires-tier: "entitled" to the 6 commands that hit registration/firm datasets gated behind a higher FINRA credential tier.
reason: The hand-written commands' Example fields were flag-only (no command-path prefix), so the live dogfood happy-path prober could not derive a runnable invocation and failed all 12 with "missing runnable example". Separately, live testing with a real basic-tier credential confirmed 6 commands (complaints new/list, registration individual/firm/timeline/validate-batch) return a clean FINRA 403 for registration/firm datasets requiring a higher entitlement tier; pp:requires-tier lets live dogfood skip them by tier instead of failing, and lets a future entitled-tier run exercise them for real via --auth-tier.
validated_outcome: Publish-time live dogfood (--level full --auth-tier basic): 74/74 runnable tests passed, 63 skipped (tier-gated + mutating fixtures), 0 failed.
